channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Lingfield and Worcester host competitive Wednesday cards
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Worcester and Lingfield are the venues for today's racing, with National Hunt and Flat action on the agenda.

item.title: Red Bull getting calls from 'good drivers' over future seat - Mekies
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Red Bull team principal Laurent Mekies has suggested they are getting calls from "good drivers" about potential future seats at the team but believes they will make the further pace improvements required to ensure Max Verstappen stays.

item.title: FRSC intensifies enforcement to curb overloading, road crashes in Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps, FRSC, Kebbi Sector Command, has intensified enforcement against overloading and other traffic violations across the state under its ongoing special operation aimed at improving road safety and reducing crashes. The Kebbi State Sector Commander, Rukayya Zakaria, disclos

item.title: 2027: Why Obi-Kwankwaso ticket is dead on arrival in Kano – Ganduje
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Abdullahi Ganduje, has dismissed the prospects of a Peter Obi-Rabiu Kwankwaso presidential ticket ahead of the 2027 general election, declaring that such an alliance would fail to gain support in Kano State. The post 2027: Why Obi-Kwan

item.title: How Kaigame wanted to use me to pay ransom – Ndume
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The lawmaker representing Borno South Senatorial District, Ali Ndume, has recounted how one Samaila Kaigame, the purported leader of Borno South Youth Alliance, wanted to use him to pay ransom for kidnapped victims. Featuring in an interview on Arise Television’s ‘Prime Time’ on&#1

item.title: Industry seeks regulatory clarity after court affirms FCCPC, NCC roles
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dapo Akinrefon ABUJA—A Federal High Court judgment affirming the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission’s, FCCPC, authority to regulate Nigeria’s airtime lending market, while holding that telecommunications licensing remains the responsibility of the Nigerian Communications Commi

item.title: PenCom to increase statutory pension contribution rates in Reform Act review
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The National Pension Commission (PenCom) has disclosed plans to increase statutory pension contribution rates as part of an ongoing review of the Pension Reform Act (PRA) 2014. The post PenCom to increase statutory pension contribution rates in Reform Act review appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Zamfara: One killed, 11 abducted travellers rescued after bandits’ attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: One person was killed and 11 abducted travellers were rescued by the Zamfara State Police Command following a bandit attack along the Gummi–Sokoto Road. The command disclosed that the attack occurred at about 10:30 a.m. on Tuesday when suspected armed bandits ambushed a trailer conveying vegetables

item.title: Court adjourns Shinkafi’s suit over Zamfara North APC primary
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal High Court in Gusau has adjourned the suit filed by an All Progressives Congress, APC, senatorial aspirant, Abdullahi Shinkafi, challenging the outcome of the party’s Zamfara North Senatorial District primary election to July 28 for the adoption of final written addresses. Just

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Wednesday morning
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Wednesday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Wednesday morning appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

item.title: Troops launch offensive, destroy IEDs, clear terrorist-prone communities in Borno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have destroyed two improvised explosive devices, IEDs, and conducted extensive clearance operations across parts of Borno State as part of ongoing wet season offensives aimed at denying Boko Haram and ISWAP terrorists freedom of movement. Security analyst and counter-in

item.title: Tinubu’s Far-Reaching Reforms Repositioning Nigeria For Long-Term Prosperity — Minister
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Information and National Orientation, Mohammed Idris, has said the far-reaching reforms introduced by President Bola Tinubu are repositioning Nigeria for long-term prosperity by strengthening economic stability, boosting investor confidence and accelerating national development. Idri

item.title: NASS primaries: Tunji-Ojo not behind Ondo APC crisis — Group
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: AKURE — THE BTO Renewed Hope Group, yesterday, dismissed allegations by a former Commissioner for Energy and Mineral Resources in Ondo State, Mr Razak Obe, that the Minister of Interior, Dr Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, is responsible for the crisis within the All Progressives Congress, APC, in the state. The

item.title: PDP Will Emerge Stronger Ahead Of 2027 – BoT Chairman
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Chairman of the Board of Trustees (BoT) of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Senator Mao Ohuabunwa, has assured members and supporters that the party had overcome its recent internal challenges and was focused on rebuilding ahead of the 2027 general elections. In a statement issued yesterday, Ohua

item.title: NIHSA issues seven-day flood alert for 17 states over rising river levels
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigeria Hydrological Services Agency (NIHSA) has issued a seven-day flood advisory, warning that 17 states face a medium flood risk between July 21 and July 27 due to rising river levels. The post NIHSA issues seven-day flood alert for 17 states over rising river levels appeared first on Nairame

item.title: Court Hears Substituted Benue APC Candidates’ Suit August 27
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja has fixed August 27 for definite hearing in a suit filed by “validly elected candidates” of the Benue State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC), challenging their substitution for the 2027 general elections. Justice Inyang Ekwo adjourned the matte

item.title: Tinubu’s wife urges Nigerians to patronise local fabrics to boost industrial revival
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The wife of the President, Mrs Oluremi Tinubu has urged Nigerians to patronise indigenously produced clothes in the country in order to help the products compete favourably with foreign brands. Mrs Tinubu, who was speaking in Akwete, Abia State on Tuesday during her inspection of Akwete fabric produ

item.title: Tinubu’s reforms repositioning Nigeria for long-term prosperity: Idris
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Idris said key achievements included the removal of fuel subsidy, foreign exchange reforms, improved fiscal capacity for the Federal Government and sub-national governments and expansion of the Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND).

item.title: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Wilson was accused of defaming young Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, but cleared by a Sydney court.

item.title: Ex-EFCC Chairman Offers Fresh Insight On War Against Money Laundering, Others
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A former chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Dr Abdulrasheed Bawa, has completed a doctoral research that examines the strengths and shortcomings of Nigeria’s anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing framework. The work earned him a Doctor of Philosoph

item.title: Survey Identifies Stable Electricity As Key To Digital Innovation In Imo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A survey conducted by an independent group has identified consistent and reliable electricity as the single most important requirement for positioning Imo State as Nigeria’s leading hub for technology, digital innovation and the knowledge economy. The finding emerged from a poll conducted by I

item.title: Akume Pushes Digital Reform In Justice Delivery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The secretary to the government of the federation (SGF), Senator George Akume, has charged the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT) to embrace digital technology in the administration of justice to enhance case management, records administration and overall operational efficiency. The SGF gave the advice

item.title: HR Must Continue To Evolve To Drive Organisational Performance – CIPM
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Abuja branch chairman of the Chartered Institute of Personnel Management, Mr Aina Segun Aina, has said human resources must continue to evolve from a traditional support function into a strategic partner that drives organisational performance, innovation, leadership, and sustainable business suc

item.title: Transfer: I’m flabbergasted – Paul Merson names two players Arsenal should sign as Trossard’s replacement
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Arsenal legend, Paul Merson, has named two players the Gunners should sign as Leandro Trossard’s replacement. Merson is not happy that Arsenal sold the Belgium international to Beşiktaş for the ‘price of a cup of tea.’ Trossard recently joined the Turkish Super Lig side Besiktas on

item.title: Experts Urge Govt To Adopt Geospatial Intelligence Against Insecurity
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Security experts have called on the federal government to make substantial investments in geospatial intelligence technology to strengthen Nigeria’s fight against terrorism, banditry and kidnapping. During a virtual dialogue organised by the WeweNetwork Afrique, they stressed that modern secur

item.title: World Cup: Scaloni addresses Messi, Martinez’s future with Argentina after Spain defeat
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina coach, Lionel Scaloni, has addressed the speculation surrounding the future of captain, Lionel Messi and goalkeeper, Emiliano Martinez with the Albiceleste following their World Cup final defeat to Spain on Sunday. Reports had emerged about the continuity of Messi and Martinez with th

item.title: EPL: What Morgan Rogers said about Palmer after joining Chelsea
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Chelsea’s new signing, Morgan Rogers, has disclosed that he knew Cole Palmer during their time together at the England youth teams and also Manchester City academy. Rogers stated this at his first interview as a Chelsea player after joining the Blues from Aston Villa on Tuesday. “I

item.title: 2027: North Central Seals Pact With Tinubu, Vows Landslide Victory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A socio-political advocacy group, North Central Agenda (NCA), has endorsed President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s re-election bid, declaring that his victory in the region during the 2027 presidential election is assured. The group attributed its position to the administration’s deliberate invest

item.title: ‘Kaduna Needs 15,000 Staff For 24-hour Health Care Services In PHCs’
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Monitoring, Evaluation, Research and Learning (MERL) manager at EngenderHealth Consortium, Dr Abdulazeez Jumare, has declared that Kaduna State needs at least 15,000 staff members in its over 1,000 primary health centres (PHCs) to provide 24-hour e services to the residents. Jumare stated this

item.title: ECOWAS reaffirms plan to rollout ECO single currency in 2027
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Economic Community of West African States, ECOWAS, has reaffirmed its commitment to launching the long-awaited single regional currency, the ECO, in 2027. According to the regional body, this is a major step towards deeper economic integration, stronger regional trade and sustainable growth acro

item.title: Transfer: Arsenal announce deal for Upson
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Arsenal on Tuesday announced that Elijah Upson has signed a professional contract with the Gunners after deciding to leave Tottenham. The 18-year-old centre-back is the son of former Arsenal defender Matthew Upson. Speaking after signing his contract with Arsenal, Upson said via the club&#

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Mallam Bolaji Abdullahi is the National Publicity Secretary of the African Democratic Congress (ADC). item.title: Nigeria losing millions in carbon credit earnings to foreign experts over talent gap — Expert warns
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Cynthia Alo Nigeria risks losing hundreds of millions of dollars in potential carbon credit earnings to foreign consultants and technical experts unless it urgently develops the skilled workforce needed to participate in the rapidly expanding global carbon market, a climate finance expert has war

item.title: Goats of football: Indian village holds tournament with an Indigenous twist
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In a remote district surrounded by forests and hills, teams from tribal villages compete for football glory - and goats.

item.title: Mexico Legend Ochoa Retires After 22-Year Career
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Guillermo Ochoa, widely considered among the greatest Mexican goalkeepers in history, announced his retirement from elite soccer on Tuesday, bringing an end to a distinguished 22-year professional career. Ochoa, who turned 41 last week, said before the 2026 World Cup that it would be his last tourna

item.title: How structural neglect weakens Nigeria’s Cocoa earnings
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Nigerian farmers losing out in $284b global cocoa marketBy Cynthia Alo Nigeria’s inability to develop a strong agricultural value chain may be denying farmers the opportunity to benefit from Cocoa and Coffee exports leaving many producers earning a fraction of revenue from the global market.

item.title: Rare Earths Giant Lynas Shares Fall to Five-Month Low After Revenue Miss
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. shares dropped to a five-month low after Australia’s largest rare-earths producer reported quarterly sales that missed analysts’ expectations.

item.title: M-East war: Almost 100 US troops injured in recent weeks — Pentagon
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Nkiruka Nnorom with agency report Nearly 100 US service members have been injured over the last two weeks of escalating strikes on Iran, the Pentagon has said. This is even as Tehran launched a wave of reprisals to the tenth consecutive night of attacks.President Donald Trump vowed to take care o

item.title: Abacha’s regime marked by brutal repression, rights abuses, says ex-DSS operative
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Former Assistant Director of the Department of State Services, Dennis Amachree, has described the regime of late military Head of State, late Gen. Sani Abacha, as one of “extreme authoritarianism and brutal repression.” The retired intelligence officer added that Abacha’s regime was marked by widesp

item.title: Tinubu approves new army battalion for Kwara
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Demola Akinyemi, Ilorin President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has approved the establishment of a new Nigerian Army battalion in Omu-Aran, Irepodun Local Government Area of Kwara State, as part of efforts to strengthen security in the state. The development was disclosed in a statement issued by the Deputy

item.title: US expands strikes on 11th straight night of attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US military’s 11th consecutive night of strikes extended to Iranian provinces not previously hit.

item.title: Sudan: Drone Attack On Ar Rahad Market, North Kordofan, Kills and Injures Children
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Unicef] Khartoum -- "A drone attack on a market in Ar Rahad, North Kordofan, yesterday, reportedly killed at least two children and injured six others, aged between six and 14. Four adults were also reportedly killed and more than 20 people injured.

item.title: Nigeria can’t scale $1trn economy on shaky terrain of weak governance — Shettima
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Nkiruka Nnorom LAGOS—Vice President Kashim Shettima has said Nigeria cannot achieve its $1 trillion economy target on the shaky terrain of weak compliance, regulatory laxity and ethical ambivalence, declaring that only a bedrock of flawless corporate governance could sustain the nation’s economic

item.title: South Africa Finance Chief Set to Remove PIC Chairman, N24 Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Africa’s finance chief is preparing to oust his deputy from the position of chairman of the board of the Public Investment Corp., the state pension fund manager that’s been wracked by corruption and instability, News24 reported.

item.title: Hegseth called a ‘failure’ over Iran as he reveals $37b cost of war
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gets into a heated exchange after being called a failed leader by Michigan Senator.

item.title: Nigeria: Nigerian Lawmakers Worry Over 53,000 Annual Deaths Linked to Unsafe Food
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The lawmakers urged the Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare, NAFDAC, SON and the NCDC to strengthen nationwide surveillance, routine inspections and certification processes covering the entire food production and distribution chain.

item.title: Africa: Africa Now World's Hunger Capital, As Global Hunger Falls for Third Year
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Vanguard] Global hunger declined for the third consecutive year in 2025, but Africa has emerged as the epicentre of the world's food crisis, with the continent now accounting for the highest number of hungry people globally, a new United Nations report has revealed.

item.title: Copper Wavers Near $14,000 as Traders Watch for Trump’s Tariff
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Copper pulled back from its highest level since early June as traders watched for any announcement from the White House on its plans for import tariffs on the metal.

item.title: Liverpool Will Sign A Winger –Iraola
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Liverpool manager Andoni Iraola has confirmed that the club definitely needs to sign another winger this summer to help cover attacking areas following Mohamed Salah’s departure. While the club recently added highly rated Victor Munoz from Osasuna, Iraola is looking for further reinforcements

item.title: Chelsea Are The Biggest Club In London —Rogers
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Chelsea new signing Morgan Rogers has described the Blues as the biggest club in London. The Premier League giants completed the signing of Rogers from Aston Villa in a British-record £117 million deal. The 23-year-old has put pen to paper on a contract running until 2033, ending interest from Arsen

item.title: Women who should not marry yet
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Marriage is not for baby girls. It is for women who are ready for the marathon. Because it is not a 100-metre dash. When Roseline complained to her grandmother that marriage was not what she expected. It was not even what my husband promised when we were dating. “Grandma,” she said, “my husband does

item.description: A ferry capsized in Guyana over the weekend, killing at least 53 people.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE sentencing to death of former Nanjing official, Yang Youlin, by a Chinese court for accepting more than 2.2 billion yuan in bribes over three decades is beyond another high-profile corruption case. It is a powerful statement by the Chinese State that public office is a sacred trust and that thos

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) says its operatives have uncovered a suspected fake university in Ilado, Olorunda LCDA of Lagos. Speaking on Monday after the raid, Gbenga Ekunola, chief superintendent and area commander of the NSCDC in Badagry, said 106 youths were rescued from

item.title: Why we are fighting digital fraud for Nigerians, empowering entrepreneurs —Opay
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ROTIMIIGE writes on how leading fintech, Opay, is championing the fight against digital fraud with innovative technology within the banking system in Nigeria, and how they are protecting trust in Africa’s largest digital economy. In West Africa, Nigerian commerce moves at a relentless, dynamic pace.

item.title: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.

item.description: A team of students has developed a futuristic solar-powered ambulance designed to provide medical assistance in remote and hard-to-reach locations. item.description: Gaza mourns as families are buried after Israeli attacks leave dozens dead, including children and displaced families.

item.title: Africa: Global Hunger Declines Again but Progress Remains Uneven Across Regions
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

item.title: Sudan: Cholera Outbreak in North Kordofan 'Contained' - Sudan's Health Minister
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica

item.title: Mauritania: UN Refugee Agency Reports 144 Migrants Dead or Missing Off Mauritanian Coast
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] The UN's refugee agency (UNHCR) reports that 144 migrants attempting to reach Europe are dead or missing due to a series of "tragic maritime incidents" off the coast of Mauritania last week.

item.title: Senegal: Senegal's Faye Backs Ex-President Sall's Bid for UN Top Job in U-Turn
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye has endorsed former President Macky Sall's candidacy to become the next United Nations secretary-general, the foreign ministry said on Monday, giving his predecessor crucial backing from his home country despite deep political divisions.

item.title: More than 140 dead, missing on refugee boat stranded off Mauritania
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.description: Wheat rose for a second session on escalating Black Sea tensions and worsening crop prospects.

item.title: Ilenikhena poised for Nigeria switch
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Al-Ittihad striker George Ilenikhena has reportedly committed his international future to Nigeria after talks with Super Eagles coach Eric Chelle.

item.title: I’m too old for Arsenal – Aina
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nottingham Forest’s Ola Aina dismisses Arsenal move, joking he’s “too old” for the Gunners. The 29-year-old also targets five goals next season.

item.title: Ondo women endorse Tinubu, target one million votes
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: WOMEN across Ondo State have endorsed President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for a second term in office, saying his administration’s economic reforms, infrastructure development and social intervention programmes have earned him another mandate in the 2027 presidential election. Women drawn from the 18 local

item.title: One year after tax reform: Shape of Tinubu’s economic foresight
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: How President Tinubu’s bold reforms and Dr Zacch Adedeji’s leadership at the Nigeria Revenue Service are driving record revenue growth The post One year after tax reform: Shape of Tinubu’s economic foresight appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News - Nigeria and World News.
